NOTICE TO PLAINTIFF

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, PLAINTIFF FREMONT GOLD MINING, LLC'S MOTION FOR DECLARATORY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE FOR LEAVE TO SERVE BY ALTERNATE MEANS. NO APPEARANCES. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DENIED. MOVING PARTY FAILS TO CITE ANY AUTHORITY FOR THE PROPOSITION THAT THE RELIEF SOUGHT (WHICH APPEARS TO BE THE SOLE RELIEF SOUGHT BY THE COMPLAINT) MAY BE OBTAINED ON A MOTION RATHER THAN TRIAL. FURTHER PLAINTIFF IS SEEKING ADVISORY OPINION FROM THE COURT AS TO HOW TO FURTHER PROCEED WITH THE ACTION. NO AUTHORITY IS CITED FOR THE PROPOSITION THAT THE COURT COULD OR SHOULD PROVIDE SUCH ADVICE TO ONE PARTY TO THE ACTION. TO THE EXTENT PLAINTIFF IS SEEKING SERVICE BY PUBLICATION, SUCH REQUEST SHOULD BE MADE TO DEPARTMENT 206. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: RONALD E. QUIDACHAY, CLERK: E. LARNAUTI, NOT REPORTED

For Declaratory Judgment Or In The Alternative For Leave To Serve By Alternate Means
Off Calendar Aug-13-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF FREMONT GOLD MINING, LLC'S MOTION FOR DECLARATORY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE FOR LEAVE TO SERVE BY ALTERNATE MEANS

No appearances. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: DENIED. Moving party fails to cite any authority for the proposition that the relief sought (which appears to be the sole relief sought by the Complaint) may be obtained on a motion rather than trial. Further Plaintiff is seeking advisory opinion from the Court as to how to further proceed with the action. No authority is cited for the proposition that the Court could or should provide such advice to one party to the action. To the extent Plaintiff is seeking service by publication, such request should be made to department 206. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (501/REQ)
